avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab will continue to develop. There will be an increased period of instability during the afternoon when avalanches will be likely on steep wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. North-Easterly aspects will also be affected by cross-loading. Cornices will be prone to collapse. Below 900 metres the snowpack will slowly consolidate. Wind exposed slopes will be firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Strong Easterly winds will back East-North-East for a few hours during the day. The freezing level will rise gradually at first from 600 metres to around 800 metres, then more quickly during the afternoon to around 1100 metres. Showery precipitation throughout the period will be heavier for a time during the morning. Falling as snow initially to 500 metres then confined to the highest summits by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Fresh snow fall overnight to low levels which was more Intermittent throughout the day. Strong Easterly winds. Freezing level around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: There has been some consolidation of recent deposits but unstable windslab is developing in wind sheltered locations on some South-West but mostly West to North aspects above 900 metres. Moderately bonded snow is also present in steep sheltered locations above 600 metres. Fragile cornices have formed above these aspects. Slopes exposed to the winds are scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Windslab will continue to develop with avalanches likely on W to N aspects above 850m. Steep locations especially affected including gullies, corrie head walls and convexities. Cross loading on SW and NE aspects.  Between 500 and 850m, the snowpack will be moderately bonded on steep banks and burn lines. Cornices will become increasingly unstable during the temperature rise. Later in the day the snowpack will begin to consolidate mainly at lower levels. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Spells of snowfall throughout the forecast period. Gale force East-South-Easterly winds will ease to strong through the afternoon. The 600 metre freezing level will steadily rise to almost summit levels by the end of the forecast period.
Observed Weather Influences: Persistent light snowfall throughout the day on strong to gale force South-Easterly winds.  The freezing level was around 600 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: New windslab has developed above 500 metres mainly on Westerly to Northerly aspects, cross loading has affected South-West and North-East aspects. The greatest instability is above 850 metres in steep locations such as coire head walls, gullies and convexities. Fragile cornices overhang these areas. Between 500 and 850 metres localised accumulations of moderately bonded windslab have developed on steep banks and burn lines. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Little change is expected in the snowpack. Areas of well bonded windslab will remain on mainly wind sheltered West to North aspects above 900 metres. On other aspects the snowpack will be mostly wind scoured, firm and stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds very strong Easterly overnight will lessen becoming fresh on the tops during the day. Freezing level will gradually rise to around 1000m during the afternoon. It is expected to be a dry day with little or no precipitation.
Observed Weather Influences: A cold day with strong Easterly winds on the tops. Some light precipitation in the afternoon. Freezing level around 500m.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of well bonded windslab are present on mainly wind sheltered West to Northerly aspects above 900 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is firm, wind scoured and stable. 
The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of unstable windslab will remain. Although not extensive, these will be poorly bonded and will tend to be found on sheltered West to North aspects above 800 metres. There will be some slow consolidation during the afternoon. On other aspects, and in wind exposed locations, the snowpack will tend to be firm and generally stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A cloudy day with a few snow showers possible. Gale force Easterly winds are expected. The freezing level will creep upward to around 1000 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cold day with strong South-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 500 metres. After some overnight snow showers on the hills it was a generally dry day.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of fresh unstable windslab were deposited in sheltered locations on mainly West to North aspects above about 800 metres. Although these areas are not extensive they tend to be poorly bonded. On other aspects, and locations which caught the wind, the snowpack tends to be scoured, firm and stable.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Any new snow deposits are not expected to be significant and will consolidate as the temperature rises in the afternoon. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Light showers will initially fall as snow above around 600 metres and clear during the afternoon as the freezing level rises to around the highest summits. Gale force Easterly winds will gradually ease through the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with gale force Easterly winds across the summits. The freezing level was around 500 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded firm and stable. Good cover on most aspects above 700 metres, more patchy at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Much of the snowpack will remain firm and icy in places with good general stability. New snow and Easterly winds will add depth to localised areas of windslab in sheltered gullies and on slopes mainly above 850 metres with a South-West through North-West to Northerly aspect. Gully exits and steep corrie rims will be most affected. Some small fragile cornices may also exist in these locations.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Light falls of new snow are possible overnight and through Saturday. Winds will remain Easterly strong to gale force overnight but reducing through the day. The freezing level will fluctuate around the summits rising to 1400 metres later in the day.
Observed Weather Influences: New snow overnight affected slopes mainly above 400 metres. Easterly winds were generally fresh to strong. Summit temperatures today are around -4 degrees C.
ObservedSnowStability: Much of the snowpack is re-frozen and firm with good general stability. Areas of moderately bonded windslab exist in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a South-West to North-Westerly aspect, these areas tend to be localised mainly to gully exits and steep corrie rims.  The avalanche hazard is Low.
